title: Industrial solid waste based construction and technical ceramics
abstract: A ceramic for construction or technical applications, composed of at least one of Municipal Solid Waste Incinerator Bottom Ash (MSWIBA) and other recycled industrial solid waste and different methods of forming such ceramics. Various techniques illustrate how ceramics are formed using at least one of extrusion shaping, dry powder compaction and agglomeration, any of which can be preceded by a pre-treatment process of received feedstock.
claims:
Claims 1. A method of producing a ceramic product comprising: receiving as a first component material a first feedstock; receiving as a second component material a second feedstock; combining the first and second component materials with water to form at least one of (1) an extrudable paste and (2) a granulated mixture; forming a green body from the at least one of (1) the extrudable paste after extrusion and (2) the granulated mixture; drying the green body; firing the green body to form the ceramic product; and cooling the ceramic product. 2. The method as claimed in claim 1, wherein the first component material is pretreated Municipal Solid Waste Incinerator Bottom Ash (MSWIBA), and wherein the second component material is Unfired Raw Ceramic Material (UCRM) powder. 3. The method as claimed in claim 2, wherein the first component is at least 30% by weight of the first and second components mixture. 4. The method as claimed in claim 2, wherein the second component is at least 10% by weight of the first and second components mixture. 5. The method as claimed in claim 2, wherein the first component is 80% by weight of the first and second components mixture, and wherein the second component is 20% by weight of the first and second components mixture. 6. The method as claimed in claim 2, wherein combining the first and second components further comprises combining a third component material to form the at least one of (1) the extrudable paste and (2) the granulated mixture, wherein the third component is selected from the group consisting of: Municipal Solid Waste Incinerator Fly Ash 

(MSWIFA), Glass Waste, Steelmaking dusts, Alumina Red Mud, Aluminum Dross, Biomass Ash, Asbestos-Containing Waste, Rock Dust, Sand, and Fired Ceramic Materials. 7. The method as claimed in claim 2, wherein combining the first and second components further comprises combining a third component and a fourth component to form the at least one of (1) the extrudable paste and (2) the granulated mixture, wherein the third component and the fourth component are selected from the group consisting of: Municipal Solid Waste Incinerator Fly Ash: (MSWIFA), Glass Waste, Steelmaking dusts, Alumina Red Mud, Aluminum Dross, Biomass Ash, Asbestos Containing Waste, Rock Dust, Sand, and Fired Ceramic Materials. 8. The method as claimed in claim 6, wherein the third component is at least 3% by weight of the first, second, and third components. 9. The method as claimed in claim 7, wherein the third and fourth components combined are at least 3% by weight of the first, second, third, and fourth components mixture. 10. The method as claimed in claim 6, wherein combining the first and second components further comprises combining a third component to form the at least one of (1) the extrudable paste and (2) the granulated mixture, wherein the third component is fired ceramic, and wherein the first, second and third components have relative weights of 70%, 25% and 5% respectively. 11. The method as claimed in claim 7, wherein combining the first and second components further comprises combining a third component and a fourth component to form the at least one of (1) the extrudable paste and (2) the granulated mixture, wherein the third component is fired ceramic, wherein the fourth component is sand, and wherein the first, second, third and fourth components have relative weights of 70%, 20%, 5%, and 5% respectively. 12. The method as claimed in claim 1, wherein combining the first and second components further comprises combining a third component to form the at least one of (1) the extrudable paste and (2) the granulated mixture, wherein the first component is Unfired Raw Ceramic Material (UCRM) powder, wherein the second component is selected between fired ceramic material powder and Sand, and wherein the third component is selected from the group consisting of Municipal Solid Waste Incinerator Bottom Ash (MSWIBA), Glass Waste, Steelmaking dusts, fired ceramic material (if not selected as the second component), and Rock Dust. 13. The method as claimed in claim 12, wherein the first component is at least 40% by weight of the first, second and third components mixture. 14. The method as claimed in claim 12, wherein the second component is at least 10% by weight of the first, second and third components mixture. 15. The method as claimed in claim 12, wherein the second component is sand, and third component is glass waste, and wherein the first, second and third components have relative weights of 60%, 15% and 25% respectively. 16. The method as claimed in claim 12, wherein the second component is sand, and third component is Fired Ceramic Material, and wherein the first, second and third components have relative weights of 80%, 10% and 10% respectively. 17. The method as claimed in claim 12, wherein combining the first, second, and third components further comprises combining a fourth component to form the at least one of (1) the extrudable paste and (2) the granulated mixture, wherein the fourth component is selected from the group consisting of: Municipal Solid Waste Incinerator Fly Ash: (MSWIFA), Alumina Red Mud, Aluminum Dross, Biomass Ash, and Steelmaking dusts. 18. The method as claimed in claim 17, wherein the fourth component has a relative weight of at least 3% with respect to the first through fourth components. 19. The method as claimed in claim 17, wherein the second component is sand, the third component is glass waste and the fourth component is Steelmaking dusts, and wherein the first, second, third , and fourth components have relative weights of 55%, 15%, 25% and 5% respectively. 20. A ceramic tile made according to the method of claim 1, wherein the first feedstock comprises at least 40% recycled materials.
